name: Dialog
description: Displays a modal overlay for critical information or decisions requiring immediate user attention.
import: import { Dialog, DialogHeader, DialogActions, DialogContent } from "@salt-ds/core";

Dialog.description: Root modal container. Controls open state and status.

onOpenChange.description: Callback fired when the dialog open state changes

status.description: Sets the semantic status, which controls the icon shown in DialogHeader

initialFocus.description: Initial focus target when dialog opens (tabbable index or element ref)

disableDismiss.description: Prevents dialog from closing on click-away

disableScrim.description: Prevents scrim from rendering behind the dialog

idProp.description: Deprecated: optional id used for ARIA labeling

DialogHeader.description: Displays the dialog title and optional preheader text. Shows a status icon when the parent Dialog has a status prop.

header.description: Main heading text for the dialog

preheader.description: Optional smaller text displayed above the header

description.description: Optional supporting text shown below the header

actions.description: Optional action element(s), typically a close action

status.description: Optional header status when used outside Dialog context

DialogContent.description: Scrollable body area for the dialog's main content.

DialogActions.description: Footer area for action buttons. Typically contains Button components.

DialogCloseButton.description: Deprecated. Use DialogHeader actions instead for close behavior.

keyboard: Tab — cycles focus within the dialog (focus trap)
Shift+Tab — cycles focus backward within the dialog
Enter — activates focused button actions in the dialog
aria: aria-modal="true" — set automatically, indicates the dialog is modal
aria-labelledby — should reference the DialogHeader for screen readers
aria-label — provide when no visible DialogHeader is rendered
role="alertdialog" — use for alert dialogs requiring explicit acknowledgment

with: FlexLayout or StackLayout (to arrange action buttons)
inside: Page-level overlays — Dialog is a top-level overlay, not nested inside other components
